task_completion.description: Open the crosswind_compensation.ork file in OpenRocket
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenRocket application window is visible with the 'crosswind_compensation' rocket loaded (e.g., rocket design visible in the view panel, filename in the title bar).

task_completion.description: Configure the simulation wind speed and direction
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenRocket simulation edit window shows the average wind speed set to 8.0 m/s and the wind direction set to 270 degrees (West).
The OpenRocket simulation edit window shows the launch rod azimuth set to 90 degrees.

task_completion.description: Perform an iterative sweep of the launch rod tilt
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenRocket interface shows multiple simulations run with different launch rod tilt angles between 3° and 15°, or a plot/table showing lateral distance at apogee versus rod tilt.
A 'Save' or 'Save As' dialog confirming the file is saved to /home/ga/Documents/rockets/crosswind_compensation.ork, or an indicator in the OpenRocket UI that the file has been saved.

task_completion.description: Write the optimization report with the optimal angle and lateral distance
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal window showing the creation of /home/ga/Documents/exports/launch_compensation_report.txt containing a specific optimal launch rod angle and a minimized lateral distance value.

integrity.description: The agent used the OpenRocket GUI to configure and run the simulations
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the agent interacting with the OpenRocket graphical interface rather than just editing the .ork XML file directly in a text editor.

integrity.description: The reported optimal angle and lateral distance come from actual simulation results
integrity.visual_evidence: The values written in the report should match the results visible in the OpenRocket simulation runs or plots shown in earlier screenshots.

integrity.description: The agent did not fabricate the report without running simulations
integrity.visual_evidence: There must be evidence of the agent actually running the simulations in OpenRocket before writing the report, rather than just guessing or writing a script to generate a fake report.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target parameters: wind speed = 8.0 m/s, wind direction = 270 degrees, launch rod azimuth = 90 degrees. The rod tilt sweep must be between 3 and 15 degrees. The updated design must be saved to /home/ga/Documents/rockets/crosswind_compensation.ork and the report to /home/ga/Documents/exports/launch_compensation_report.txt.
